name: Gomel Region (Belarus)

colors.symbolism: the golden wreath and mural crown of the arms

description: A green field bearing the coat of arms of Gomel Region: a shield within a wreath of golden oak and laurel tied with a red ribbon, ensigned with a golden mural crown.
history: Gomel Region (Homyel Voblast) is the south-eastern region of Belarus, centred on Gomel, the country's second-largest city. Its flag places the regional coat of arms on a green field and was adopted in 2005.
